meta.name: GapSight Knowledge Base
meta.description: Metrics-driven regulatory gap mapping for EU AI Act, NIST AI RMF, and ISO/IEC 42001. GapSight — AI regulatory self-assessment for ML teams. Use this as context when building GapSight compliance checklist logic. For each metric the user inputs, map it to the requirements below and return pass/review/fail status.
meta.threshold_policy: None of the three frameworks prescribe numeric thresholds. GapSight uses industry-reasonable defaults. Users can override thresholds.
meta.target_user: Small ML teams, AI startups, freelance ML engineers building or deploying AI systems
meta.tagline: AI regulatory self-assessment for ML teams.
meta.disclaimer: GapSight is an informative self-assessment tool. Not legal advice. Not a compliance platform. Thresholds are GapSight industry defaults, not regulatory requirements.

metrics.label: Overall Accuracy
metrics.description: Proportion of correct predictions over total predictions.

covers.requirement: High-risk AI systems must achieve appropriate levels of accuracy. Providers must declare accuracy metrics in instructions for use.
covers.evidence_required: Test report showing accuracy on representative test set
covers.status_logic: pass if >= pass_threshold, review if >= review_threshold, fail if below

covers.requirement: Performance criteria are measured quantitatively under deployment-like conditions and results are documented.
covers.evidence_required: Metrics report with accuracy, F1, AUC in deployment-representative conditions

covers.requirement: Organisations must monitor KPIs including accuracy. Verification and validation must test against performance metrics.
covers.evidence_required: Documented KPI monitoring and test results

metrics.label: F1 Score (weighted)
metrics.description: Harmonic mean of precision and recall. Especially relevant for imbalanced datasets.

covers.requirement: Validation and testing must document metrics for accuracy. Annex IV requires documentation of metrics used.
covers.evidence_required: Test report including F1 score by class and overall

covers.requirement: Risk-appropriate metrics are selected and implemented. F1 score is listed as an example metric for performance evaluation.
covers.evidence_required: Metric selection justification and test results

covers.requirement: Performance monitoring must use valid metrics. F1 score supports verification and validation requirements.
covers.evidence_required: Performance evaluation report

metrics.label: AUC-ROC
metrics.description: Area under the receiver operating characteristic curve. Measures discriminative ability.

covers.requirement: Providers must document metrics used for accuracy and robustness in Annex IV technical documentation.
covers.evidence_required: ROC curve and AUC value in test report

covers.requirement: Validity and reliability must be demonstrated. AUC is an accepted metric for classification model reliability.
covers.evidence_required: Test documentation with AUC and interpretation

covers.requirement: KPI monitoring must use robust methodologies and valid metrics.
covers.evidence_required: Documented evaluation methodology including AUC

metrics.label: Demographic Parity Difference
metrics.description: Difference in positive prediction rates between protected and reference groups. Closer to 0 is better.

covers.requirement: Training, validation and testing datasets must be bias-free. Providers must detect and mitigate bias. Annex IV requires documenting validation metrics for discriminatory impacts.
covers.evidence_required: Fairness audit report showing demographic parity difference per protected group

covers.requirement: Fairness and bias are evaluated and results documented. Demographic parity difference is an explicit example metric.
covers.evidence_required: Bias evaluation report with demographic parity results and mitigation actions

covers.requirement: Bias assessment is required during data preparation. Testing and validation must include fairness metrics.
covers.evidence_required: Data preparation bias assessment and fairness test results

metrics.label: Equalized Odds Difference
metrics.description: Difference in true positive and false positive rates between groups. Closer to 0 is better.

covers.requirement: Providers must detect and mitigate bias. Article 13 requires reporting performance for specific groups.
covers.evidence_required: Fairness report showing equalized odds per protected group

covers.requirement: Equalized odds difference is an explicit example metric for fairness evaluation.
covers.evidence_required: Fairness metrics report with equalized odds and mitigation plan

covers.reference: Annex A.6.4, Annex A.5.4, Annex A.5.5
covers.requirement: Bias testing and validation against fairness metrics are required. Clause 9 expects fairness KPI monitoring.
covers.evidence_required: Fairness evaluation documented in performance monitoring

metrics.label: Data Drift Score
metrics.description: Statistical distance between training data distribution and live data distribution. Lower means less drift.

covers.requirement: High-risk systems must prevent feedback loops and perform consistently throughout their lifecycle. Logging must enable post-market monitoring.
covers.evidence_required: Drift monitoring logs and alert thresholds

covers.requirement: Risks are tracked over time. System behavior is monitored in production. Drift detection is implicitly required for consistent performance.
covers.evidence_required: Continuous monitoring plan and drift score logs

covers.requirement: Deployment and operation controls must detect drift and ensure fairness and robustness over time.
covers.evidence_required: Drift monitoring evidence and response procedures

metrics.label: Concept Drift Score
metrics.description: Measure of change in the relationship between input features and target labels over time.

covers.requirement: Systems that continue to learn after deployment must prevent biased feedback loops. Consistent performance throughout lifecycle is required.
covers.evidence_required: Concept drift monitoring logs and retraining procedures

covers.requirement: Track emergent risks including concept drift. User feedback must be integrated into evaluation.
covers.evidence_required: Drift tracking plan and corrective action records

covers.requirement: Performance monitoring must detect concept drift as part of deployment and operation controls.
covers.evidence_required: Documented monitoring methodology and drift response plan

metrics.label: Adversarial Robustness Score
metrics.description: Model accuracy under adversarial attack conditions (e.g., FGSM, PGD). Higher is better.

covers.requirement: Providers must implement cybersecurity measures against adversarial inputs, data poisoning and model evasion attacks.
covers.evidence_required: Adversarial robustness test report with attack scenarios and results

covers.requirement: Security and resilience are evaluated. Adversarial testing results are an explicit evidence requirement.
covers.evidence_required: Penetration testing and adversarial evaluation reports

covers.requirement: Verification and validation must include robustness testing. Operation controls must ensure robustness.
covers.evidence_required: Robustness test documentation including adversarial scenarios

metrics.label: Explainability Coverage (SHAP)
metrics.description: Proportion of model predictions for which valid SHAP or feature importance explanations are available.

covers.requirement: Providers must state whether the system can be explained and provide information for deployers. Annex IV requires documentation of model logic and assumptions.
covers.evidence_required: Model card with SHAP values or feature importance documentation

covers.requirement: Model is explained, validated and documented. Outputs are interpreted in context. SHAP values are an explicit example.
covers.evidence_required: Explainability report with SHAP values and user-facing interpretation

covers.requirement: Technical documentation must include model architecture, assumptions and limitations. Transparency to stakeholders is required.
covers.evidence_required: Model card and stakeholder documentation with explanations

metrics.label: Human Oversight Documented
metrics.description: Whether human oversight procedures are formally documented including override and intervention mechanisms.

covers.requirement: High-risk systems must be designed for effective human oversight. Operators must be able to understand, monitor, override and halt the system. Automation bias must be addressed.
covers.evidence_required: Human oversight plan, operator training records, documented override procedures

covers.requirement: Safety risks are evaluated. Human oversight processes are defined and documented.
covers.evidence_required: Oversight procedure documentation and training evidence

covers.reference: Annex A.5.6, Annex A.5.7, Annex A.8.3
covers.requirement: Human oversight must be incorporated in design, deployment and operation. Human review is required for high-stakes decisions.
covers.evidence_required: Documented oversight procedures and evidence of operator training

process_requirements.description: These requirements cannot be satisfied by metrics alone. They require documentation, policies or organisational processes. GapSight should flag these as separate action items.

items.label: Risk Management System
items.description: Formal risk management process with risk identification, analysis, evaluation, mitigation and iterative updates.
items.evidence_required: Risk register, impact assessment reports, hazard analysis, mitigation measures

items.label: Technical Documentation (Annex IV)
items.description: Complete technical documentation including system description, architecture, data governance, validation reports, and post-market monitoring plan.
items.evidence_required: Completed Annex IV document, model cards, data lineage records, architecture diagrams

items.label: AI Governance Policy
items.description: Documented AI policy approved by leadership covering fairness, transparency, human oversight and safety.
items.evidence_required: Signed AI policy document, role assignments, accountability framework

items.label: Data Governance Policy
items.description: Documented procedures for data collection, quality, provenance, preparation and bias mitigation.
items.evidence_required: Data governance policy, data lineage records, bias mitigation procedures

items.label: Automated Logging and Record Keeping
items.description: Automatic logging of events to enable risk identification, post-market monitoring and traceability.
items.evidence_required: Logging architecture, stored logs, incident records

items.label: Quality Management System
items.description: Documented quality management system covering design, development, testing, validation, risk management, post-market monitoring and incident reporting.
items.evidence_required: Quality management manual, internal audit reports, corrective action logs

items.label: Third-Party and Supply Chain Management
items.description: Assessment and monitoring of third-party AI systems, pre-trained models and data suppliers.
items.evidence_required: Vendor assessments, contracts with AI responsibilities, supplier performance reviews

status_logic.pass: Metric meets or exceeds pass threshold. Requirement is covered with appropriate evidence.
status_logic.review: Metric is between review and pass threshold. Requirement is partially addressed but needs attention.
status_logic.fail: Metric is below review threshold or not provided. Requirement is not covered.
status_logic.not_applicable: Metric or requirement does not apply to this system type.
status_logic.process_required: This requirement cannot be satisfied by metrics alone. Documentation or organisational process is needed.

sme_provisions.description: EU AI Act includes simplified provisions for SMEs and startups.
items: SMEs may use simplified Annex IV technical documentation template.
Micro-enterprises may adopt simplified quality management system.
SMEs have priority access to regulatory sandboxes at reduced fees.
Penalties are proportional: whichever is lower between fixed amount and percentage of turnover.

compliance_deadlines.obligation: Prohibited AI practices banned. AI literacy obligations in effect.
GPAI model obligations apply. Technical documentation, training data summaries, copyright policy required for GPAI providers.
Full EU AI Act applies. High-risk system requirements (Articles 8-15) mandatory. Transparency obligations for limited-risk systems.
Extended transition deadline for high-risk AI in regulated products.

tagline: AI regulatory self-assessment for ML teams.
